data.title: Trump Officials Push to Slow Nvidia, AMD Chip Deliveries to Middle East
data.description:
Some senior Trump officials have grown worried about the speed of which Trump has signed chip deals with countries in the Middle East, according to Bloomberg. In particular, they are worried that chips delivered to the Middle East could benefit China and that speedy deals will lead to faulty guardrails. Some officials also question shipping out large quantities of chips anywhere, as it could threaten the AI leadership status of the U.S.

Middle East Chip Deals Could Help China, Says Trump Officials

Earlier this week, the U.S. secured a $600 billion investment commitment from Saudi Arabia, which includes a massive order for Nvidia (NVDA) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) chips. The U.S. is also considering sending over a million Nvidia chips to the United Arab Emirates.

Nvidia currently tops TipRank's list of Most Active Stocks.

data.title: July Fed Rate Cut Odds Rise on Soft PPI Report
data.description:
Traders are now assigning a 35.9% chance that the Fed will cut rates by 25 basis points (bps) during the July meeting, up from 32.5% a day ago. They also believe there is a 61.4% that the Fed holds rates steady between 4.25% and 4.50%, down from 65.1% a day ago. The latest PPI report shows that inflation could be lower than expected, supporting a rate cut.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Quickly and easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks straight to you inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

Producer Price Index Posts Biggest Drop Since 2020

The producer price index (PPI) measures the average change that producers receive for their goods and services. On a month-over-month basis, the April PPI fell by 0.5%, way below the expectation for a rise of 0.2%. The fall represents the largest decline since the pandemic in 2020. The PPI rose by 2.4% on a year-over-year basis, below the expectation for 2.5%.

At the same time, the steep drop can be traced to services and a few products. For example, egg prices dropped by 39% month-over-month while services prices fell by 0.7%, the largest drop since at least 2009.

Keep up to date with the latest economic metrics using TipRank's Economic Indicators Dashboard.

data.title: M&A News: Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS) Eyes Foot Locker in $2.3B Deal
data.description:
Sports retail chain Dick's Sporting Goods is in advanced talks to acquire athletic footwear retailer Foot Locker (FL) in a deal valued at $2.3 billion, the Wall Street Journal reported. The acquisition is expected to give Dick's a stronger foothold in footwear and boost global presence. News of the potential buyout sent Foot Locker stock soaring 69.3% in after-hours trading, while DKS stock was down 5.5%.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Quickly and easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks straight to you inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

It must be noted that Dick's is looking to offer $24 per share for Foot Locker. This potential offer reflects a premium of nearly 90% over Foot Locker's closing price of $12.87 on Wednesday.

The news follows a recent agreement by sneaker maker Skechers (SKX) to be acquired by private equity firm 3G Capital in a $9.4 billion deal earlier this month, which points to an ongoing consolidation within the athletic and footwear retail market.

Why Is Dick's Buying Foot Locker?

Foot Locker has been making turnaround efforts to boost sales, such as store revamps and a better rewards program. Despite this, Foot Locker's revenue has dropped for the past three years, falling below $8 billion for the year ended February 1, as U.S. consumers cut spending.

Foot Locker stock is down 40% year-to-date, partly due to the tariff concerns and Nike's (NKE) strategic price changes aimed at increasing its own sales.

For Dick's, acquiring Foot Locker would mean instant access to 2,400 stores worldwide, compared to its 800 big-box locations in the U.S. This expansion could help Dick's diversify its revenue streams and compete better with brands like Nike and Adidas (DE:ADS).

Is DKS a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, DKS stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 10 Buys and nine Holds assigned in the last three months. At $229.69, the average Dick's price target implies a 9.58% upside potential.

See more DKS analyst ratings

data.title: Rocket Lab Stock (RKLB) Soars After Record-Breaking Back-to-Back Earth Return Missions
data.description:
Rocket Lab USA (RKLB) stock surged over 10% on Wednesday after the company announced that one of its Pioneer spacecraft, built for Varda Space Industries, successfully returned to Earth. This marks the company's second Earth return mission in just two months. Celebrating the achievement, CEO Peter Beck praised its Pioneer spacecraft for its "fast re-entry, adaptability, and dependability."

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Quickly and easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks straight to you inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

For context, Rocket Lab specializes in the development and launch of small satellite launch vehicles.

Rocket Lab's Fast-Paced Returns

Rocket Lab announced that, for the second time in two months, it has successfully supported a Varda mission using its Electron rocket and Pioneer spacecraft. The mission involved launching Varda's W-3 space capsule into Earth orbit and safely bringing it back to the ground.

Notably, Varda is a California-based company focused on orbital pharmaceutical production and hypersonic re-entry logistics. Varda has partnered with Rocket Lab for a series of four missions that include both launch and return phases. The latest mission is the third overall and the second completed this year.

Additionally, the company stated that the fourth spacecraft in Varda's W-series is now being assembled and tested at its Spacecraft Production Complex.

What This Means for Investors

Rocket Lab USA's recent success in completing Earth return missions for Varda reinforces its strategy to expand into full-spectrum space services. With a growing launch frequency, the company is positioning itself to meet rising demand in the space sector.

These steps could boost revenue and profit growth in the next few years as Rocket Lab builds its place in the medium-lift launch market. However, challenges in execution and supply chains are key risks that could affect its financial path.

Since last week, several analysts have shared their views on Rocket Lab after the company reported better-than-expected first-quarter results. Recently, Needham analysts reaffirmed their Buy rating and maintained a $28 price target. Needham highlighted the company's strong progress with its Neutron rocket, expected to launch later this year. Meanwhile, analysts at Stifel Nicolaus and Cantor Fitzgerald raised their price targets on RKLB stock with Buy ratings.

Is RKLB a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, RKLB stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with nine Buys and four Holds assigned in the last three months. The average Rocket Lab stock price target is $28.55, suggesting a potential upside of 18.3% from the current level.

See more RKLB analyst ratings

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 5/15/25 – Futures Dip After Three-Day SPX Rally
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged lower early Thursday, after the S&P 500 (SPX) recorded its third consecutive day of gains. This positive momentum was largely due to the easing of the U.S.-China trade tensions. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 were down 0.51%, 0.54%, and 0.46%, respectively, at 4:12 a.m. EST, May 15.

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

  • Discover companies with rock-solid fundamentals in TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter.
  • Receive undervalued stocks, resilient to market uncertainty, delivered straight to your inbox.

During Wednesday's regular trading session, the Dow Jones edged down by 0.21%. In contrast, the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite (NDAQ) rose 0.1% and 0.72%, respectively.

Particularly, tech stocks showed notable momentum. Nvidia (NVDA) rallied by 4.2% following its AI chip deal with Saudi Arabia. Also, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) climbed 4.7% after announcing a $6 billion stock buyback program.

In after-hours trading, Foot Locker's (FL) shares soared over 60% following reports that Dick's Sporting Goods (DKS) is nearing a deal to acquire FL for about $2.3 billion. In contrast, UnitedHealth (UNH) stock slid 8% following news that the U.S. Justice Department is probing the insurer for potential Medicare fraud.

Looking ahead, traders are awaiting the release of April's Producer Price Index (PPI), Retail Sales, and Industrial Production reports, as well as the Weekly Jobless Claims data, for further insights into the economy's health.

At the same time, earnings from Alibaba (BABA), Walmart (WMT), Applied Materials (AMAT), Virgin Galactic (SPCE), Take-Two Interactive (TTWO), Deere (DE), and Quantum Computing (QUBT) are also for release today.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year treasury yield was down, floating near 4.518%. Simultaneously, WTI crude oil futures are trending lower, hovering near $61.08 per barrel as of the last check.

At the same time, the Gold Spot U.S. Dollar Price fell to a one-month low of nearly $3,140 per ounce on Thursday, after tensions eased between the U.S. and China, reducing demand for safe-haven assets.

Elsewhere, European indices opened lower today, as investors looked for fresh market drivers following the U.S.-China trade deal boost.

Asia-Pacific Markets Ended Lower Today

Asia-Pacific indices were in the red today, as traders kept a close watch on U.S.-China trade talks.

At the same time, the Hong Kong index was down 0.88%. Also, China's Shenzhen Component and Shanghai Composite indices closed lower by 1.62% and 0.68%, respectively. Further, Japan's Topix and Nikkei indices declined 0.88% and 0.98%, respectively.

Interested in more economic insights? Tune in to our LIVE webinar.

data.title: Stock Market News Today – SPY and QQQ Stock Close Higher on U.S.-Qatar Commitment, Additional Trade Deal Whispers
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) closed higher on Wednesday, adding on their positive year-to-date returns following a strong reversal from their April lows.

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

  • Discover companies with rock-solid fundamentals in TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter.
  • Receive undervalued stocks, resilient to market uncertainty, delivered straight to your inbox.

This morning, Trump met with Syrian President Ahmed Al-Sharaa, one day after he promised to remove sanctions on the country. During the meeting, Trump urged Sharaa to sign the Abraham Accords, an agreement that establishes normalization policies with Israel.

In a trade update, China moved forward to suspend export controls for 28 firms and adjusted its unreliable entities list for a number of U.S. companies.

Additionally, the U.S. is reportedly "close" to signing trade deals with Japan and Korea, according to Fox Business senior correspondent Charles Gasparino. India is also nearing a deal, which has been slow to materialize given the India-Pakistan conflict.

Meanwhile, Boeing (BA) reached a new intraday 52-week high after the U.S. agreed to a $1.2 trillion economic exchange and $243.5 billion deal with Qatar. As part of the deal, Qatar Airways has agreed to purchase up to 210 787 Dreamliner and 777X aircraft. The deal also benefits technology, energy, and defense industries within both countries.

Finally, the CEO of Hapag-Lloyd, an international shipping and container transportation company, stated that China to U.S. containership bookings have jumped by 50% following the U.S.-China 90-day tariff pause.

The S&P 500 finished Wednesday with a 0.10% gain while the Nasdaq 100 fared better with a 0.57% jump.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: China to U.S. Containership Bookings Increase by 50%, Says International Shipping CEO
data.description:
After the U.S. and China agreed to mutually lower tariffs for 90 days, China to U.S. containership bookings have jumped by over 50% compared to the last four weeks, said Hapag-Lloyd CEO Rolf Habben Jansen.

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

  • Discover companies with rock-solid fundamentals in TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter.
  • Receive undervalued stocks, resilient to market uncertainty, delivered straight to your inbox.

China to U.S. Shipments Surge on Tariff Reduction

Hapag-Lloyd, a German international shipping and container lines company, also experienced a double-digit increase in volume. Jansen added that it's possible that customers will front-load shipments ahead of the 90-day grace period deadline.

The SonicShares Global Shipping ETF (BOAT) has jumped by 8% during the past five days as a result of the U.S.-China tariff pause.

data.title: S&P 500 Index Today: SPY Inches Higher on $1.2 Trillion U.S.-Qatar Commitment, Aramco Deals
data.description:
The S&P 500 (SPX) is trading slightly higher after the benchmark index erased all of its year-to-date losses on Tuesday.

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

  • Discover companies with rock-solid fundamentals in TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter.
  • Receive undervalued stocks, resilient to market uncertainty, delivered straight to your inbox.

Earlier today, the U.S. inked a $1.2 trillion economic exchange agreement with Qatar, as well as deals worth over $243.5 billion. S&P 500 company Boeing (BA) was a major beneficiary of the deal with Qatar agreeing to buy up to 210 of its 787 Dreamliner and 777X aircraft.

Adding on to Middle East deal is an up to $90 billion agreement between Saudi Arabia's Aramco and 34 U.S. companies, which include Nvidia (NVDA) and Amazon (AMZN).

What's more, the U.S. is also "close" to signing trade deals with Japan and Korea, according to Fox Business senior correspondent Charles Gasparino. A U.S-India deal is also being worked on, although the country's conflict with Pakistan has resulted in a speed bump.

The S&P 500 is up by 0.09% at the time of writing.

Which Stocks are Moving the S&P 500?

Let's take a look at TipRank's S&P 500 Heatmap, which shows the stocks that have contributed to the index's price movement.

Nvidia and Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) stand out as solid winners, both with gains over 3%. Tesla also stands out with a 4% gain while healthcare stocks continue to drag down the index following Trump's executive order on drug prices. Meanwhile, the S&P 500's industrial stocks are noticeably muted.

SPY Stock Moves Higher with the S&P 500

The SPDR S&P 500 ETF (SPY) is an exchange-traded fund created to track the movement of the S&P 500.

Wall Street believes that SPY should trade higher. During the past three months, analysts have issued an average SPY price target of $648.62 for the stocks within the index, implying upside of 10.42% from current prices. The 504 stocks in the SPY carry 416 buy ratings, 79 hold ratings, and 9 sell ratings.

data.title: Dow Jones Index Today: DIA Stock Continues Decline Despite Boeing-Qatar Deal, Strong Nvidia Performance
data.description:
Following a 0.88% fall on Tuesday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) is yet again in the red. Yesterday's decline was largely attributed to a significant 18% drop in UnitedHealth Group (UNH) after its CEO, Andrew Witty, stepped down. In addition, the multinational healthcare company also suspended its guidance for 2025. UNH stock carries a 4.54% weight in the Dow Jones and is the index's seventh largest component.

Protect Your Portfolio Against Market Uncertainty

Meanwhile, Boeing (BA), which has a 2.97% weight in the index as its 18th largest holding, received a historic order this morning. Qatar has agreed to buy up to 210 of the company's 787 Dreamliner and 777X aircraft, sending BA stock to new 52-week highs.

The Dow Jones is down by 0.37% at the time of writing.

Which Stocks are Moving the Dow Jones?

Let's take a look at TipRank's Dow Jones Heatmap, which illustrates the stocks that have contributed to the index's price action.

Nvidia (NVDA) is leading all Dow Jones companies with a 3.29% gain while healthcare stocks Amgen (AMGN), Merck (MRK), and Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) are all in the red. Meanwhile, Boeing is the only industrials stock registering a gain.

DIA Stock Moves Higher with the Dow Jones

The SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF (DIA) is an exchange-traded fund designed to track the movement of the Dow Jones. As such, DIA is falling in correlation with the Dow Jones today.

Wall Street believes that DIA stock has room to rise. During the past three months, analysts have issued an average DIA price target of $477.06 for the stocks within the index, implying upside of 13.66% from current prices. The 31 stocks in the DIA carry 30 buy ratings, 1 hold rating, and zero sell ratings.

data.title: Trumps Locks in $1.2 Trillion Economic Exchange, $243 Billion Deal With Qatar
data.description:
After announcing a $600 billion investment commitment from Saudi Arabia on Tuesday, President Trump has secured another deal in the Middle East. Today, Trump signed an agreement with Qatar that will result in an economic exchange worth at least $1.2 trillion, according to a White House fact sheet. The U.S. has also signed a deal with Qatar worth over $243.5 billion that will benefit technology, energy, and defense companies.

Confident Investing Starts Here:

  • Quickly and easily unpack a company's performance with TipRanks' new KPI Data for smart investment decisions
  • Receive undervalued, market resilient stocks straight to you inbox with TipRanks' Smart Value Newsletter

Trump Signs Another Deal in the Middle East

As part of the deal, Qatar Airways has agreed to a $96 billion agreement with Boeing (BA) for up to 210 787 Dreamliner and 777X aircraft. BA stock reached a new 52-week high on the news.

Furthermore, Qatar will receive advanced military equipment from Raytheon (RTX) and General Atomics. The deal also includes partnerships between McDermott and Qatar Energy, as well as quantum computer company Quantinuum and Al Rabban Capital.

"These new agreements and instruments aim to drive the growth of the U.S.-Qatar bilateral commercial relationship, create thousands of well-paying jobs, and open new trade and investment opportunities for both countries over the coming decade and beyond," said the White House.
